rulings.Case Title: DOMINIC REA VS. SUSAN BAILEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendant Susan A. Bailey Md To Dismiss This Action Due To Plaintiff'S Failure To Serve The Complaint Within Three Years, Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 583.210 - 583.250
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 14, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT SUSAN BAILEY, M.D.'s Motion To Dismiss This Action Due To Plaintiff'S Failure To Serve The Complaint Within Three Years, Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 583.210 - 583.250. Defendant Susan Bailey's motion to dismiss this action due to plaintiff's failure to serve the complaint within three years is granted. Mr. Rea has not shown that his failure to serve Dr. Bailey within three years of filing this case was impossible, impracticable or futile due to causes beyond his control. While the court is very sympathetic to Mr. Rea's physical and mental health issues, Mr. Rea has not shown that any of those issues prevented him from serving Dr. Bailey within the mandatory three year period. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the Dr. Bailey is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: TERRY BUTTS VS. FARMERS INSURANCE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 14, 2018, Line 11, DEFENDANT TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE, MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.(part 1 of 2 of tentative ruling) Hearing required unless counsel for defendants sends an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to counsel for Mr. Butts stating that Mr. Butts does not feel the need for any further argument on the motion. The court re-affirms the tentative ruling issued prior to the August 7 hearing: Defendants Truck Insurance Exchange and Mid-Century Insurance Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Per Elkinton v. California State Automobile Association, Interstate Insurance Bureau (1959) 173 Cal.App.2d 338, 344, which remains good law, only slight evidence is necessary to find implied permission to operate a vehicle where a parent-child relationship is involved. (See also Pierce v. Standow (1958) 163 Cal.App.2d 286 (finding implied permission where the mother gave the keys to her son even though the mother expressly told the son not to drive the car).) No later than their receipt of the judicial arbitration award in the underlying case, and likely much before then, defendants knew of the existence of possibly provable facts that viewed in the light most favorable to coverage showed that at the very least there was a possibility of coverage under the policies issued by MCIC and Truck. In the words of the arbitrator, those facts included that "the parents [of Christian Somerville] knew that¿[Mr. Somerville] had a propensity to try and take whatever it was he wanted," Mr. Somerville "had taken and passed a driving course ¿ and had expressed interest to his parents in obtaining his permit/driver's license," and "[e]ven when his parents had discovered that¿[Mr. Somerville] had taken items from their bedroom, they¿did not lock their bedroom door or dresser drawer where¿[Mr. Somerville]¿obtained the 'spare keys' to the Suburban." As many cases have held, '[i]f coverage depends on an unresolved dispute over a factual question, the very existence of that dispute would establish a possibility of coverage and thus a duty to defend.'" (North Counties Engineering, Inc. v. State Farm General Ins. Co. (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 902, 922.) Moreover, Mr. Scherrer's letter of June 6, 2013 stating that "we have identified coverage issues that will require additional investigation" is a concession that there is a possibility of coverage which itself triggered a duty to defend. The fact that the letter was written with regard to the Vance and Allen claims and prior to the tender of the Mr. Butts' complaint in the underlying action is immaterial since the same coverage dispute was involved in all three claims. The insurers' heavy reliance on Government Employees Insurance Company v. Kinyon (1981) 119 Cal. App. 3d 213 and Mucci v. Winter (1951) 103 Cal. App. 2d 627 is misplaced. Neither case involved an issue of implied permission by a parent to a child driver, and Mucci was expressly distinguished on that point by Elkinton. While the policy language in Kinyon is similar to the policy language in the MCIC policy, the insurers ignore that the subjective belief of the driver test enunciated in that case is not mutually exclusive of the "inferable state of mind of the alleged permitter" test that applies to determining implied permission. That latter test, which did not apply to Kinyon because that was a non-owned vehicle case, is applicable to this case due to Insurance Code 11580.1(b)(4), which statutorily mandates coverage for any driver who had the implied permission of the named insured with respect to an owned vehicle of the named insured. (end of part 1 of 2)=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: TERRY BUTTS VS. FARMERS INSURANCE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Truck Insurance Exchange, Mid-Century Insurance Company Motion For Summary Judgment. (For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 14, 2018, Line 11, Defendant Truck Insurance Exchange, Mid-Century Insurance Company Motion For Summary Judgment. (part 2 of 2) (For Tentative Ruling Only) Based on that test, even if not the subjective belief test, there is a triable dispute whether the possibly provable facts known to MCIC and Truck by the time they received the arbitration award created a possibility of coverage, thereby triggering a duty to defend Mr. Somerville. Farmers Insurance and Farmers Insurance Group's request for summary judgment in their favor is granted. Mr. Giles' declaration which showed that "Farmers Insurance" and "Farmers Insurance Group" are merely trademark names and they are not subject to being sued establishes that there is no dispute that those trademark names cannot be held liable for any of the claims asserted by Mr. Butts in this case. Mr. Butts' objections to Mr. Giles' declaration are overruled. Counsel for Mr. Butts is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: TERRY BUTTS VS. FARMERS INSURANCE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 14, 2018, Line 11, DEFENDANT TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE, MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Hearing required unless counsel for defendants sends an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to counsel for Mr. Butts stating that Mr. Butts does not feel the need for any further argument on the motion. The court re-affirms the tentative ruling issued prior to the August 7 hearing: Defendants Truck Insurance Exchange and Mid-Century Insurance Company's motion for summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Defendants fail to show that Mr. Butts cannot establish his bad faith claim. While the genuine dispute doctrine might apply to any dispute over the applicable legal test to determine coverage, it does not apply to the parties' dispute about the provable facts bearing on whether Christian Somerville had the implied permission of his parents to drive the Suburban. Since there are both legal and factual disputes in this case, MCIC and Truck cannot avail themselves of the genuine dispute doctrine. (See Mt. Hawley Insurance Company. v. Lopez (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 1385, 1424, n. 20.) Moreover, there is a triable dispute whether the insurers conducted a reasonable investigation into the possibly provable facts, especially after Mr. Scherrer's letter of June 6, 2013 stating that "we have identified coverage issues that will require additional investigation" and their receipt of the arbitration award in the underlying case. There are also triable disputes whether the insurers had fraudulent intent regarding their communications with Mr. Somerville and whether they acted with malice or oppression, as those terms are defined in Civil Code 3294(c), by clear and convincing evidence. Based on the possibly provable facts known to the insurers by the time they received the arbitration award, and likely much before then, there are triable issues whether their denial of a duty to defend was known to them to be fraudulent and constituted despicable conduct in willful disregard of the rights of Mr. Somerville and Mr. Butts and causing cruel and unjust hardship to one or both of them. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Butts is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: CHEW KAM KUNG VS. JINCONG ZHEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 14, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT JINCONG ZHEN, SAM WING CONSTRUCTION, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Jincong Zhen and Sam Wing Construction, Inc.'s demurrer to all four causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Chew Kam Kung is overruled as to the third cause of action for construction defects against Mr. Zhen and sustained with thirty days leave to amend as to the first, second and fourth causes of action for breach of contract, unlicensed contractor and slander of title against both Mr. Zhen and Sam Wing and the third cause of action against Sam Wing. Liberally construed, Mr. Kung's third cause of action for construction defects alleges a viable negligence claim against Mr. Zhen. Mr. Kung is given one last opportunity to allege any other valid claims he believes he has against Mr. Zhen and Sam Wing. Mr. Kung should review the April 25, 2018 order as to what he must allege for a viable breach of contract claim. For an unlicensed contractor claim, Mr. Kung must allege the construction work requiring a license that he claims was performed by an unlicensed contractor, who was the unlicensed contractor who performed the work and what damages he is seeking. Mr. Kung's third cause of action does not allege any negligent conduct by Sam Wing. For a slander of title claim, Mr. Kung must allege what actions constitute a slander of title. While it appears that Mr. Kung might be able to validly allege all of the claims he wishes to pursue against Mr. Zhen and Sam Wing, Mr. Kung's apparent difficulties with writing in English appears to be an obstacle to what he is seeking to achieve. The court suggests that, if Mr. Kung is unable to find an attorney to represent him, perhaps he might seek the help of someone who can help him with writing in English. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Mr. Zhen and Sam Wing is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: VINCENZO JOSEPH CORBELLI VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Opposing Dylan A. Mcmahans Good Faith Application For Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 14, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion Opposing Dylan A. Mcmahans Good Faith Application For Settlement. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to contest defendant Dylan McMahan's application for determination of the good faith of his settlement with plaintiff Vincenzo Corbelli is denied. Mr. McMahan appears to acknowlege that his settlement is not within the reasonable range of his proportionate share of liability for Mr. Corbelli's claimed harm and thus seeks a good faith imprimatur of the settlement solely based on the settlement for the limits of his insurance policy and his personal financial condition. However, unlike other settlements where the settlor's financial condition strongly suggested very limited or no ability of the settlor to contribute any more than the amount of the settlement, Mr. McMahan's personal financial condition suggests that he has the financial capacity to make some (albeit perhaps not large in percentage terms of Mr. Corbelli's total claimed harm) non-trivial personal contribution to a settlement with Mr. Corbelli. (Compare Aero-Crete, Inc. v. Superior Court (1993) 21 Cal. App. 4th 203; County of Los Angeles v. Guerrero (1989) 209 Cal. App. 3d 1149; Schmid v. Superior Court (1988) 205 Cal. App. 3d 1244). Moreover, neither Mr. McMahan nor his counsel has explicitly stated whether the settlement provides for any cooperation by Mr. McMahan in Mr. Corbelli's claims against the remaining defendants. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: CHARTER ASSET MANAGMENT FUND, L.P. VS. LATIN GRAMMAR SCHOOL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Determine Prevailing Party And Fix Attorneys Fees As Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 14, 2018, Line 12, DEFENDANT LATIN GRAMMAR SCHOOL, INC., LATIN COLLEGE PREPARATORY CHARTER SCHOOL, INC.'s Motion To Determine Prevailing Party And Fix Attorneys Fees As Costs. Hearing required. The court re-affirms the tentative hearing issued prior to the August 7 hearing: Defendants Latin Grammar School, Inc. and Latin College Preparatory Charter School, Inc.'s motion for attorneys' fees is denied. Defendants' successful motion to quash was not a determination on the merits of the case, but solely a ruling on whether they are subject to being sued in California on the claims of plaintiff Charter Asset Management Fund, L.P. The findings made on the motion to quash were made for the sole purpose of determining whether this court could exercise specific personal jurisdiction over defendants and were not intended to and presumably are not binding on any merits issue, although that issue may need to be litigated in Georgia. Because the merits of the contract claims have not been determined and CAM has stated that it intends to pursue those claims in Georgia, defendants are not prevailing parties for purposes of a fees award. Counsel for CAM is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: PETER ENGLANDER VS. BICYCLE TOOLS INCORPORATED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories; Form Interrogatories; Request For Production From Bicycle Tools Incorporated, And For Order Establishing Admissions, And Request For Sanctions.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 14, 2018, Line 2 PLAINTIFF PETER ENGLANDER'S Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories; Form Interrogatories; Request For Production From Bicycle Tools Incorporated, And For Order Establishing Admissions, And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Peter Englander's motion to compel responses to special interrogatories, form interrogatories, request for production, and for an order establishing admissions is granted. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted. No opposition was filed. Prior to the hearing, and even if the tentative ruling is not contested, counsel for plaintiff Peter Englander is required to prepare a proposed order which includes the statement that the motion was not opposed and email the proposed order to contestdept302tr@sftc.org; mtderish@gmail.com; and opposing counsel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mtderish@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CRANA WINDOWS & DOORS, INC. VS. THOMPSON BROOKS INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Writ Of Attachment Judith A. Thompson
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 14, 2018, Line 10, PLAINTIFF CRANA WINDOWS & DOORS, INC.'s Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Writ Of Attachment Judith A. Thompson. Plaintiff Crana Windows & Doors' application for a right to attach order and writ of attachment against defendant Thompson Brooks Inc. is off calendar. The automatic bankruptcy precludes this court from ruling on the application as to TBI. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: CRANA WINDOWS & DOORS, INC. VS. THOMPSON BROOKS INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Writ Of Attachment Thompson Brooks
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 14, 2018, Line 10, PLAINTIFF CRANA WINDOWS & DOORS, INC.'s Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Writ Of Attachment Thompson Brooks. Plaintiff Crana Windows & Doors' application for a right to attach order and writ of attachment against defendant Judith Thompson is is denied. McLaughlin has failed to establish the probable validity of any of its contract claims against Ms. Thompson since it has not shown that Ms. Thompson entered into any contract with McLaughlin or that Ms. Thompson is the alter ego of and thus liable for the debts of TBI. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Thompson is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)
